summarization.a: The line chart shows the relationship between gap size and relative intermixing ratio at two positions, B and C

a.question: Why is the relative intermixing ratio much higher at position C than at position B?
a.answer: Position C (TMA side) experiences higher intermixing because water has a higher inlet vapor pressure than TMA, resulting in stronger diffusive transport of water molecules toward the TMA zone. At 1 mm gap, the relative intermixing ratio at position C is approximately 0.25 (1:4 water to TMA), while position B shows only about 0.01. This asymmetry reflects the different volatilities of the two precursors.

a.question: How is the relative intermixing ratio defined in this figure?
a.answer: It is defined as the concentration ratio of the foreign gas to the home gas at each monitoring position.

a.question: What happens to intermixing at position C when gap size increases from 1 mm to 2 mm?
a.answer: The relative intermixing ratio decreases dramatically from approximately 0.25 to 0.03, representing roughly an 8-fold reduction.

a.question: Based on this figure, is precursor intermixing a significant concern for a 2 mm gap spatial ALD reactor?
a.answer: No. At 2 mm gap size, both positions show low intermixing ratios (below 0.03), indicating effective precursor separation with minimal risk of unwanted CVD-type reactions.
